Patch Set 5:

This tests that the DoM code doesn't break any existing functionality, but is it actually testing DoM layouts? 

It would be useful to include a change in one of the earlier patches that sets a default layout with DoM and PFL to verify that all of the failover and revxovery functionality is working.

Ran 15 tests.  5 tests failed: replay-single, mmp, recovery-small, recovery-random-scale, recovery-mds-scale.
Abandoned

parents.subject: LU-3285 mds: combine DoM bit with other IBITS

commit.subject: LU-0000 tests: verify dom with full and failover test groups
commit.message:
LU-0000 tests: verify dom with full and failover test groups

This is for test only.

Test-Parameters: fortestonly \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes \
mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs \
clientdistro=el7 ossdistro=el7 mdsdistro=el7 \
mdscount=1 mdtcount=1 testgroup=full

Test-Parameters: fortestonly \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes \
mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs \
clientdistro=el7 ossdistro=el7 mdsdistro=el7 \
mdscount=2 mdtcount=4 testgroup=full

Test-Parameters: fortestonly \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes,REQFAIL=100 \
mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs \
clientdistro=el7 ossdistro=el7 mdsdistro=el7 \
clientcount=4 osscount=2 mdscount=2 mdtcount=1 \
austeroptions=-R failover=true iscsi=1 \
testgroup=failover

Change-Id: Ie58b55a096c4ff4c884d658b8cae70baa84d522d
Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
